description_long: This is a stone Celtic cross monument mounted on a rectangular pedestal. The cross features a circular ring connecting its arms, with intricate carved patterns along the edges of both the cross and the ring. The pedestal is solid and tapers slightly upward, with an engraved plaque or inscription area on its front face. The entire sculpture is rendered in a uniform gray stone texture, giving it a solemn and enduring appearance. The craftsmanship suggests a commemorative or memorial function, typical of grave markers or historical monuments. The structure is robust and appears to be made from a single block of stone.
description: A gray stone Celtic cross monument with a circular ring and detailed carvings, mounted on a solid rectangular pedestal.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the cross, the circular ring, and the engraved plaque on the pedestal.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the cross and pedestal, with no visible engravings or details.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, showing the back of the cross and pedestal.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thickness and profile of the cross and pedestal.
attribute_location_context: No specific context is visible, but the monument is typical of outdoor urban or park settings.
